elements.name: Title
texts: "Three boatloads of Dublin’s unemployed came in"

elements.name: Description
texts: The poetry of Patrick Kavanagh's 'Lough Derg', detailing a visit in 1941

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts:
“Time passed
Three boatloads of Dublin’s unemployed came in
At three o’clock led by a priest from Thomas Street
To glutton over the peat-filled water
And sit back drunk when jobs are found
In the eternal factory where the boss
Himself must punch the clock.”
